channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Banning books prompts emotional testimony on both sides of proposed Nevada law
item.description: Parents, political groups and a member of the Clark County school board slammed legislation that is aimed at ending local decisions to pull books off school library shelves during a legislative hearing in Carson City on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Army adjusts tracker policy and flight training following January collision with passenger jet
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Army has made changes to how its helicopters use a safety system that broadcasts aircraft location and has reduced the number of flights over Washington following a collision with a passenger jet that killed 67 people, the head of Army aviation told The Associated Press on Tues
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump trade rep takes bipartisan fire over tariffs as Dem launches bid to halt them
item.description: USTR Jamieson Greer was the witness at a Senate Finance Committee hearing on Tuesday after markets appeared to rebound on tariff negotiation news
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House GOP leaders prep vote on budget despite rift within the party
item.description: Republicans in Congress are closer to passing key elements of President Trump's legislative agenda — like extending tax cuts that expire at the end of the year — but only if the House and Senate can get on the same page.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt, MPR News

item.title: Mike Huckabee nomination advances in Senate, one step closer to becoming ambassador to Israel
item.description: The Senate moved closer to confirming Mike Huckabee as ambassador to Israel amid backlash over his past remarks on Palestinians and Trump’s vision for the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court blocks order requiring Trump administration to reinstate thousands of federal workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Tuesday blocked an order for the Trump administration to return to work thousands of federal employees who were let go in mass firings aimed at dramatically downsizing the federal government.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: National Weather Service no longer translating products for non-English speakers
item.description: The National Weather Service is no longer providing language translations of its products, a change that experts say could put non-English speakers at risk of missing potentially life-saving warnings about extreme weather.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms Trump’s controversial pick for Pentagon No. 3 job
item.description: The Senate voted Tuesday to confirm Elbridge Colby, President Trump’s “lightning rod” pick to serve as the Pentagon’s under secretary for policy, despite the private concerns of several Republican senators about Colby’s past statements and views.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Dem accuses Trump admin of 'fire sale approach' as GOP aims to lower federal gov's real estate portfolio
item.description: Democrats accused the Trump administration of "looting" the federal government during a Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) subcommittee hearing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Joe Rogan, Dave Portnoy and Ben Shapiro are among Trump backers now questioning tariffs
item.description: As President Donald Trump’s tariffs roil global markets, some of the thought leaders and influential podcasters who backed the Republican’s campaign are voicing doubts.
item.dc:creator: Meg Kinnard, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump admin unveils plan costing migrants massive fine for every day they don't self-deport
item.description: The Trump administration is planning to issue a daily fine of nearly $1,000 to illegal immigrants who do not self-deport after receiving final orders to leave the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New audit, renewed criticism of Met Council's oversight of Southwest light rail project
item.description: The release of a new audit of the Southwest Corridor light rail project in the Twin Cities prompted sharp questions and bipartisan criticism from Minnesota lawmakers on Monday. Officials with the Metropolitan Council said the line is on track to begin service in 2027.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: U.S. stocks are volatile as selloffs sweep through global markets
item.description: Shares nosedived around the world Monday as higher U.S. tariffs and a backlash from Beijing triggered massive sell-offs. Trump has defended the tariffs: “You have to take medicine to fix something.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman and John Ruwitch, MPR News

item.title: Florida Rep. slams 'government-funded kidnapping program' after UF student is detained by ICE
item.description: A Florida Representative is calling out President Donald Trump and his administration for running a "government-funded kidnapping program" following ICE's recent detainment of a University of Florida student.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Conservative website‘s bid to access Sen. Mitchell body camera footage revived by appeals court
item.description: Alpha News sought to compel law enforcement to release certain video related to Sen. Nicole Mitchell’s arrest in April 2024. An appeals court ruling has reopened the possibility of body and dash camera footage being released ahead of her trial.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Will Trump tariffs bring jobs back to the US? Economists are skeptical
item.description: Economists on both sides of the aisle are skeptical that Trump's tariffs will lead to more factory jobs in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Vargas, NewsNation

item.title: Chief Justice Roberts pauses deadline for return of Maryland man mistakenly deported to El Salvador
item.description: Chief Justice John Roberts has agreed to pause a midnight deadline for the Trump administration to return a Maryland man mistakenly deported to a notorious prison in El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Freedom of speech is shifting under the Trump administration. We’re exploring how
item.description: Many Americans worry freedom of speech is fading, while others feel empowered to say what they want. NPR's Morning Edition explores this dynamic in a new series, The State of the First Amendment.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel, Arezou Rezvani, Taylor Haney, and Kyle Mackie, MPR News

item.title: TikTok creators go from sadness and fear to...'whatever!' over app's future
item.description: It's been an emotional rollercoaster for TikTok creators over the past few months, with the app's future uncertain. But there are ways to decompress.
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman, MPR News

item.title: How automakers are responding to the 25% car tariffs so far
item.description: It's only been a few days since President Trump's 25 percent tariffs on foreign cars took effect. But the import surcharge has led to some immediate changes, including layoffs and pauses in car shipments.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bowman, MPR News

item.title: Trump closes loophole used by American shoppers to buy Chinese goods tariff-free
item.description: The so-called de minimis exemption allowed Chinese and Hong Kong retailers to ship millions of packages worth $800 or less directly to U.S. consumers. That loophole will close May 2.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann, MPR News

item.title: Farmers fear tariffs could cost them one of their biggest markets in China
item.description: Heading into this year, most U.S. farmers were hoping to break even or maybe record a small profit. But now they are faced with losing the biggest export market for many of their crops after China retaliated against President Donald Trump’s tariffs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Despite backlash, Florida county term limit bill advances in the House
item.description: Florida voters may have the chance to set term limits, not just for state lawmakers, but also for county commissioners and school board members.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pentagon watchdog to review Hegseth’s use of Signal app to convey plans for Houthi strike
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon’s acting inspector general announced Thursday that he would review Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s use of the Signal messaging app to convey plans for a military strike against Houthi militants in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Staff working on childhood lead exposure and cancer clusters fired from CDC
item.description: Staff members who fought childhood lead exposure and those who worked on cancer clusters were among those fired from the Department of Health and Human Services.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Here's how newly imposed tariffs will affect California products
item.description: President Donald Trump’s widespread tariffs could have a profound impact on California’s exports, according to a study from the Public Policy Institute of California.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna clashes with GOP House leaders, plans tele-town hall
item.description: The Pinellas County Republican is pushing to allow new parents the ability to vote by proxy, or remotely, for up to 12 weeks after having a baby.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: E-Verify legislation starts to move at the statehouse, passing its first committee
item.description: The state legislature is now at the halfway point in the 2025 legislative session and as the end swiftly approaches, lawmakers in Tallahassee are pushing for more immigration reform.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New bill seeks to alleviate burden on Florida citrus growers as orange groves disappear
item.description: As Florida citrus growers continue to face hardships, causing some to make the difficult decision to sell their orange groves, a new piece of bipartisan legislation is aiming to alleviate some of the burden.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Potential Trump tariff plan could cost households thousands: new analysis
item.description: A new analysis from The Budget Lab at Yale shows how much a broad tariff plan could cost U.S. households.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, WFLA, NBC
